creative_brief: Split-screen visual: left side shows a long, winding conveyor belt with six generic toll-booth style checkpoints between a farm and a kitchen table. Right side shows a single clean line from farm to a FarmThru hub. Muted, documentary tone. No text overlay beyond the FarmThru logo.
primary_text:
If a farmer grows your food and you eat it, why do six middlemen get paid between those two moments?

Honest answer: because that's how the system was built. Not broken, just old.

FarmThru is building something different. A farm-direct grocery store where NSW families order online and collect from Brookvale. Partner farms across the state. No subscriptions, no lock-in.

A shorter supply chain that pays farmers more and charges families less.

Now you can own a piece of it.
headline: Six middlemen. Zero of them grow food.
description: Farm-direct grocery with a shorter chain. Be part of what replaces the old one.
